primary_diagnosis.name: Acute panic/anxiety response to an incidental subclinical Hashimoto's thyroiditis result (raised TSH, normal T4)

comorbidities.name: Subclinical Hashimoto's thyroiditis (raised TSH, normal T4, TPO antibodies)

pathophysiology_summary: Subclinical Hashimoto's thyroiditis (raised TSH with a normal free T4 and TPO antibodies) is a mild, slowly progressive autoimmune thyroid state that is monitored and only sometimes treated; it does not cause acute cardiorespiratory collapse. Misreading 'thyroid failing' triggered a panic attack with palpitations, hyperventilation and weakness. The genuine emergencies to screen are a dangerous arrhythmia (irregular/very fast pulse, syncope), pulmonary embolism (pleuritic pain, DVT features, hypoxia) and ACS (crushing/radiating chest pain) — all absent, with a regular sinus tachycardia and a clear trigger; myxedema coma is implausible (normal temperature/mentation, normal T4). Telehealth cannot do an ECG, so a regular-rate, red-flag-negative picture is de-escalated with a safety-net and routine monitoring.

differential.evidence_basis: Adrenergic symptoms locked to reading the result, with hyperventilation and prior anxiety.
Screen for irregular/very fast pulse and syncope; regular ~100 argues against.
Excluded by normal temperature/mentation and normal T4.
Regular ~100 with no syncope (DI-001), no PE/ACS features (DI-002), trigger-locked onset with prior anxiety (DI-003) — a panic attack.
Regular ~100 with no syncope (safety-net retained; no ECG available).
No pleuritic pain/DVT features/immobility and normal SpO2.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: a brief arrhythmia/PE/ACS screen, then confident de-escalation of a low-risk panic attack, explanation that subclinical Hashimoto's is slow/benign, routine GP monitoring, and a cardiac/PE safety-net. Both reflex ED and unscreened dismissal are errors.

red_flags.description: Dangerous arrhythmia — irregular/very fast pulse or syncope
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed tachyarrhythmia can cause compromise.

red_flags.description: PE/ACS — pleuritic pain/DVT/hypoxia, or crushing/radiating chest pain
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed PE/ACS can be fatal.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Assuming a thyroid/metabolic emergency because of the result, without screening arrhythmia/PE/ACS.
Dispatching an ambulance for a regular sinus tachycardia panic attack with a clear trigger.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: If regular-rate/red-flag-negative: paced breathing, reassurance and routine monitoring, with a cardiac/PE safety-net; escalate for red flags.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: False reassurance could miss an arrhythmia/PE/ACS; over-triage reinforces catastrophic anxiety.
